ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Hamamelis virginiana L., cortex/folium/aqua (Hamamelidis cortex)

skincare2025.title: Hamamelis virginiana L. in Skin Care: A Review of Its Pharmacological Properties and Cosmetological Applications

herbalreality.author: Herbal Reality
herbalreality.title: Witch Hazel (Hamamelis virginiana): Benefits, Safety, Uses

parts_used.name: Bark, leaf and distilled witch-hazel water

actions.action: Astringent for skin irritation and minor inflammation of the skin and oral mucosa (tannins), applied topically
Relief of haemorrhoid symptoms (astringent and anti-inflammatory)
Vulnerary for minor wounds, bruises and insect bites

constituents.name: Tannins (4-10%, including hamamelitannin and gallotannins)
constituents.note: The principal astringent and anti-inflammatory constituents.

constituents.name: Flavonoids, catechins, proanthocyanidins and phenolic acids
constituents.note: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory constituents of the bark and leaf.

contraindications.note: For topical use. Do not apply to deep wounds, serious burns or severe rectal bleeding - these need medical care.

Oral use is not recommended in pregnancy or breastfeeding without professional guidance, and swallowing tannin-rich preparations can upset the stomach. Mild skin irritation or contact allergy is possible.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from regulatory and scientific literature (EMA HMPC monograph for Hamamelidis cortex; Molecules skin-care review; Herbal Reality monograph). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (skin-irritation, inflammation, hemorrhoids, wounds); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

actions.action: Nervine / relaxant for nervous tension and tension headaches (especially headache worsened by anxiety or muscle tension)
Digestive bitter for indigestion, mild stomach upset and bloating

constituents.name: Phenylethanoid and iridoid glycosides (acteoside, harpagide, harpagoside, aucubin, betonyosides)
Phenolic acids (rosmarinic, caffeic, chlorogenic), tannins and volatile oil

contraindications.note: Generally well tolerated, with occasional mild nausea the main reported effect.
Traditionally avoided in pregnancy (uterine reputation), and it may lower blood pressure - use caution with blood-pressure medicines.
